reasoning: Reject — wrong domain entirely. This candidate is a Salesforce DevOps Engineer with zero overlap with the embedded/automotive software engineering role being hired for. The JD demands C programming, MISRA guidelines, AUTOSAR, RTOS, and RADAR system knowledge — none of which appear anywhere in this resume. The candidate's entire 5-year career is built on Salesforce CI/CD tooling (Copado, Jenkins, GitHub Actions, SFDX) and cloud-based release engineering — a completely different technology stack and domain. There is no salvageable partial match here. Do not shortlist; reject immediately and move to next candidate.
strengths: SALESFORCE DEVOPS DEPTH: Five years of end-to-end Salesforce release engineering across Copado, Jenkins, GitHub Actions, and SFDX — strong in its own domain but entirely irrelevant to this embedded automotive role.

CERTIFICATION PORTFOLIO: Holds seven certifications including Copado Certified Consultant, Flosum Certified Professional, and Salesforce Certified Administrator — demonstrates structured learning, but none applicable to C/AUTOSAR/RTOS development.

QUANTIFIED DELIVERY OUTCOMES: Documented 30-40% deployment time reduction and improved deployment success rates — shows results-orientation, but the domain is Salesforce, not embedded or automotive software.
gaps: COMPLETE DOMAIN MISMATCH — C, MISRA, AUTOSAR, RTOS, RADAR: The candidate has zero exposure to embedded C development, automotive coding standards, or any AUTOSAR/RTOS/RADAR technology. This is not a gap that can be trained quickly — it represents a fundamentally different engineering discipline. This alone is disqualifying.

NO AUTOMOTIVE OR EMBEDDED EXPERIENCE WHATSOEVER: The entire career history is Salesforce cloud DevOps. There is no EE systems background, no firmware or driver-level work, and no signal of transitioning into automotive embedded software. The B.Tech in EEE is the only tangential link, but it is 5+ years old with no applied evidence.

scoring_factors.reason: No notice period specified and candidate is currently employed; no penalty applied as JD has no stated maximum.
Candidate is based in Chennai, matching the JD's in-office Chennai requirement — no location penalty.
Candidate has 5 years total experience, which exceeds the minimum threshold of 2 years (half of 4 required) — no penalty, though experience is in the wrong domain.
JD gender preference is 'Any' — no penalty applied.
No college preference or exclusion specified in the JD — no adjustment applied.

hard_filter_failures: Domain mismatch — candidate has zero relevant technical skills for an embedded automotive SW Developer role (no C, no AUTOSAR, no RTOS, no RADAR)
availability_reason: Candidate is currently employed at Capgemini (May 2025 – Present) with no notice period stated on the resume.
alternative_role_fit: Senior Salesforce DevOps Engineer / Salesforce Release Engineer
rare_assets: Flosum Certified Professional — a rare certification for a niche Salesforce DevOps tool held by very few practitioners in the market.
Multi-tool Salesforce DevOps stack coverage (Copado, Gearset, Flosum, DevOps Center) — unusual breadth across competing platforms.
self_reported_unverified: 'Best Performer Award' and 'Special Achievement Award' — no context on team size, scope, or verifying authority provided.
30-40% deployment time reduction claim — no baseline deployment count, time frame, or business context given to validate the scale of impact.
interview_flags: Probe whether the candidate has any embedded C or automotive software exposure from their B.Tech EEE background or personal projects — the degree is the only possible link.

Ask why the candidate is applying for an embedded automotive SW Developer role given an entirely Salesforce-focused career — understand motivation and whether there has been any self-study in C or AUTOSAR.
Clarify current notice period at Capgemini — role started May 2025 and current date context matters for availability.
Ask about the transition from TCS (Salesforce DevOps) to Capgemini (also Salesforce DevOps) — verify whether any embedded or systems work was undertaken in between.
Probe depth of the 'Copado Certified Consultant' credential — consultant-level cert suggests client-facing advisory experience, but resume reads more like an internal engineer; clarify actual scope.
